new_classes.label_en: other vinegar (not wine vinegar) in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2209 00 91)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Essig (kein Weinessig) in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von höchstens 2 Litern (KN 2209 00 91)
new_classes.definition_en: Vinegar and substitutes for vinegar obtained from acetic acid, other than wine vinegar, presented in containers holding 2 litres or less. Includes vinegar obtained from acetic fermentation of alcoholic liquids other than wine (e.g. cider vinegar, malt vinegar, spirit vinegar), as well as diluted acetic acid used as vinegar, as described in HS Explanatory Note to heading 2209, part (I) second paragraph (2), (3) and (4), and part (II).

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the two main distinguishing criteria for CN 2209 00 91: (1) the product is 'other' vinegar — i.e. not wine vinegar — expressed via a complement restriction against eucn:WineVinegar; (2) the container size is ≤ 2 L, expressed via a someValuesFrom restriction on BFO:has_part targeting eucn:SmallContainer; and (3) it is produced by an acetic fermentation process, expressed via hasAceticFermentationProcess. However, the HS Explanatory Note categories referenced (part I second paragraph items 2–4 and part II) include: (2) vinegar from cider, beer, whey, etc. (acetic fermentation of non-wine alcoholic liquids); (3) spirit vinegar (from distilled alcohol); (4) other vinegar from acetic fermentation; and part (II) synthetic/diluted acetic acid substitutes for vinegar. The current TBox only has AceticFermentationProcess for fermentation-derived vinegar, but lacks a class for synthetic acetic acid / diluted acetic acid vinegar substitutes (HS 2209 part II). Additionally, the specific non-wine fermentation feedstocks (cider, beer, whey, etc.) are not distinguished from spirit vinegar in the axioms. The sibling code 22090099 (containers > 2 L) is not modelled here. Coverage is partial due to these gaps.
